description:
You are an MLOps engineer who just received a workspace that already contains a group of finished
    training runs.  
    The directory layout you will find when you open the terminal is

        /home/user/mlops/
        ├── experiments/
        │   ├── EXP001/
        │   │   ├── metrics.txt
        │   │   ├── params.yaml
        │   │   └── artifact_list.txt
        │   ├── EXP002/
        │   │   ├── metrics.txt
        │   │   ├── params.yaml
        │   │   └── artifact_list.txt
        │   └── EXP003/
        │       ├── metrics.txt
        │       ├── params.yaml
        │       └── artifact_list.txt

    Your task is to inspect the three experiments, extract a few key pieces of information,
    and create a single, human-readable *pipe-separated* log file that the whole data-science
    team can use.

    1. Create a new directory called /home/user/mlops/reports  
       (create it only if it is not already present).

    2. Inside that directory create a file named  
       /home/user/mlops/reports/experiment_summary.log

    3. The file **must follow exactly** this layout:

       • The very first line is a header line and must be written literally as

            ExperimentID|Date|Model|Accuracy|Loss|ParamLines

       • Each subsequent line represents one experiment, with the fields
         in the order shown in the header and separated by the single character “|”.
         No extra spaces are allowed around the field separators.

       • The lines **must be sorted in ascending alphabetical order of ExperimentID**
         (i.e. EXP001, EXP002, …).

       • “Date” is taken from the FIRST line of artifact_list.txt  
         (this line contains only the date string).

       • “Model” is the value that comes after “model:” in params.yaml  
         (strip any leading/trailing spaces).

       • “Accuracy” and “Loss” are the numeric values in metrics.txt that follow the
         labels “Accuracy:” and “Loss:”.

       • “ParamLines” is the number of non-empty lines that are present in params.yaml.

    4. End-of-line handling: every line (including the last one) must be terminated by the
       normal UNIX newline character “\n”.

    5. When you are done, you can check your work by displaying the content of the log file
       in the terminal.  The automated grader will read the file from the exact path given
       above and compare it byte-for-byte with the expected answer, so make sure the format
       is followed precisely.
truth:
The workspace already contains these files with the following **exact** content
    (each “\n” indicates a single newline character, and every file ends with one trailing newline).

    1. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP001/metrics.txt  
       Accuracy: 0.9321\n
       Loss: 0.1278\n

    2. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP001/params.yaml  
       model: ResNet50\n
       lr: 0.001\n
       batch_size: 32\n

    3. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP001/artifact_list.txt  
       2023-08-21\n
       checkpoint.pt\n
       tensorboard/\n

    4. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP002/metrics.txt  
       Accuracy: 0.8876\n
       Loss: 0.2345\n

    5. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP002/params.yaml  
       model: EfficientNetB0\n
       lr: 0.0005\n
       batch_size: 64\n
       augment: true\n

    6. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP002/artifact_list.txt  
       2023-08-22\n
       checkpoint.pt\n
       tensorboard/\n

    7. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP003/metrics.txt  
       Accuracy: 0.9458\n
       Loss: 0.0987\n

    8. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP003/params.yaml  
       model: ViT-B16\n
       lr: 0.0001\n
       batch_size: 16\n
       patch_size: 16\n
       epochs: 30\n

    9. /home/user/mlops/experiments/EXP003/artifact_list.txt  
       2023-08-25\n
       checkpoint.pt\n
       tensorboard/\n

    ------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected final output file:
    /home/user/mlops/reports/experiment_summary.log  
    with exactly these four lines (each followed by a newline):

        ExperimentID|Date|Model|Accuracy|Loss|ParamLines\n
        EXP001|2023-08-21|ResNet50|0.9321|0.1278|3\n
        EXP002|2023-08-22|EfficientNetB0|0.8876|0.2345|4\n
        EXP003|2023-08-25|ViT-B16|0.9458|0.0987|5\n
